title: NPR: At Least Six People Killed by Immigration Agents Since Trump's Second Term Began
excerpt: A comprehensive NPR investigation confirmed that at least six people had been killed by federal immigration agents since the start of Trump's second term, with Ruben Ray Martinez identified as the first victim—his death concealed for nearly a year. Minnesota state officials continued to be denied access to training records and personnel files for the agents involved in the Minneapolis shootings.
content:
On April 15, 2026, NPR published an in-depth investigation into the pattern of fatal shootings by ICE and CBP agents, anchored by the previously concealed killing of Ruben Ray Martinez and the ongoing federal probe evasion in the Minneapolis shootings.

  • Ruben Ray Martinez, shot March 15, 2025, is considered the first of at least six people killed by immigration agents since Trump's second term began, according to The Trace, a nonprofit focused on gun violence coverage.
  • ICE withheld its involvement in Martinez's death for nearly 11 months. The victim's identity was only confirmed after American Oversight's FOIA lawsuit forced release of internal ICE incident reports.
  • State investigators in Minnesota seeking to conduct independent reviews of the Good and Pretti shootings were still being denied access to evidence, including training and personnel records of the agents who fired.
  • Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty specifically cited the need to determine the training history of ICE Agent Jonathan Ross, who shot Good: 'When was he trained? What was he trained to do? What's in his personnel file?'
  • The DOJ declined to respond to NPR's inquiry about the status of the Pretti investigation. DHS confirmed to NPR that an 'internal investigation' was underway into the Sosa-Celis (Venezuelan man) shooting—months after two agents in that case were already placed on leave for making 'untruthful statements.'
mediaPlaceholder: NPR audio report featuring interview with Rachel Reyes, mother of Ruben Ray Martinez, and analysis of the pattern of ICE shootings
